title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. loriferum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. loriferum, a lesser known subspecies of hawkweed, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family known for its bright yellow flowers and feathery leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. loriferum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and promote the healing of burns and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of teas or tinctures, are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent to extract its medicinal properties.
